title: podman 1.9 no longer works with hidepid=2 of /proc
body:
While podman 1.8 worked fine with hidepid=2 for /proc. podman 1.9 always prints:

```
# podman info
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs    
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs  
...
```

comments.body:
@giuseppe PTAL.
I wonder if there is an easy way to add a test for this?

it seems to work here.

Do you have any conf file under ~/.config/containers?

Please share the full output for `podman --log-level debug info`

@giuseppe :
My fault it is a problem with systemd if i boot with hidepid=2. If i remount with hidepid=2 everything works fine.

When booting with hidepid=2 systemd logs:
```
Apr 20 09:52:10 host systemd[974]: Failed to determine supported controllers: No such process
Apr 20 09:52:10 host systemd[974]: Failed to allocate manager object: No such process
Apr 20 09:52:10 host systemd[1]: user@1000.service: Failed with result 'protocol'.
Apr 20 09:52:10 host systemd[1]: Failed to start User Manager for UID 1000.
```

So this is rather a problem with systemd sorry about that.
